prompt: Translate this English proverb into two other languages in a way that preserves the spirit, not the words: 'The best time to plant a tree was twenty years ago. The second best time is now.' Add a one-sentence note on each.
response:
Chinese: 种树的最佳时间是二十年前.其次佳时节是现在. (Translation: The best time to plant a tree was 20 years ago. The second-best time is now.)
[Note: This translation uses simple language to convey the time and sequence of planting a tree.]

German: Die beste Zeit, einen Baum zu pflanzen, war vor zwanzig Jahren. Die zweitbeste Zeit ist jetzt. 
(Translation: The best time to plant a tree was 20 years ago. The second-best time is now.)
[Note: This translation uses straightforward German to express the time and sequence of planting a tree, while also maintaining the tone of the original English proverb.]

Action: For each translation, I have provided a note that explains how it preserves the spirit of the original English proverb while using simple and straightforward language in the target Chinese and German languages. Please let me know if you need clarification or have any other questions.
